title: Lebanon: Images of a Nation Uprooted by War
summary: A million people in Lebanon have fled their homes as Israel clashes anew with Hezbollah. This is what their lives look now.

title: Trump Attacks Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly Over Iran War Criticism
summary: In a lengthy social media post, the president attacked Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly and others in starkly personal terms. He also criticized the editorial board of The Wall Street Journal.

title: A Record Jump in U.S. Gasoline Prices Is Squeezing Consumers
summary: The cost at the pump made its biggest monthly percentage increase in decades amid lingering tensions over the war in Iran.

title: Soaring Fuel Prices Squeeze China’s Frugal Truckers
summary: Many long-haul truckers in China have a tight budget and live frugally on the road, sleeping and cooking in their vehicles. Now, the pain at the pump is forcing some drivers to rethink their lives.

title: U.S. Inflation Surged in March as Iran War Pushed Up Prices
summary: Soaring energy costs led to the biggest monthly increase in the Consumer Price Index since the peak of the post-pandemic inflation crisis in June 2022.

title: Iran War Drives Deeper Oil Shock Than Prices Reveal
summary: The war with Iran is preventing huge amounts of oil from flowing out of the Persian Gulf, but the prices that many people track don’t fully capture the scale of the disruption.

title: Why Investing in Wind and Solar to Avoid Gas Shocks Hasn’t Added Up for Some
summary: Renewable energy is cheaper to run than fossil fuels, especially with war choking oil supply. But it hasn’t turned out that way for some European countries, and the reason is complex.

title: Tracking 7 of Mamdani’s Biggest Campaign Promises
summary: Zohran Mamdani based his successful campaign for mayor of New York City on several central pledges. Here’s an early look on where those stand.

title: Trump Posts Graphic Video of Deadly Attack and Rails Against Haitian Immigrants
summary: The man seen beating a woman is from Haiti and faces a murder charge in Florida, officials said. President Trump has fought to end protections for Haitian immigrants.

title: Judge Declines to Order Trump Administration to Adjust Somali Deportation Policies
summary: Acknowledging that the government may have recently stepped up the pace of removal proceedings, a federal judge wrote that he could not immediately intervene.

title: The Next Phase of the Immigration Crackdown Is Quieter — and More Destabilizing
summary: Self-deportation is an idea with deep roots. It’s making a comeback.

title: Who Is Keeping These Trains Moving? Teenagers, Illegally.
summary: Inside the world of “conquesting,” where vandals break into the operator’s booth, fool around with the control panel — and maybe take a quick joyride.

title: Harris Says She Is ‘Thinking About’ Running for President Again in 2028
summary: At a convention in New York, possible candidates for the Democratic presidential primary are making overtures to Black voters, a vital constituency.

title: Masked Men in San Francisco Offer Cash for Signatures on Ballot Initiatives
summary: California’s ballot initiative wars are so lucrative that signature gatherers are offering cash and pizza for names on a petition. The exchange is illegal, and state officials say they are investigating.

title: Melania Trump Says She Was Not Associated With Jeffrey Epstein
summary: Responding to what she said were smears, the first lady said she never had knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse and was not a victim of his. She called for a congressional hearing for his victims.

title: Molotov Cocktail Is Hurled at Home of OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman
summary: The device burned an exterior gate of the house in San Francisco, the police said, and it was unclear whether the artificial intelligence executive was home. The authorities arrested a suspect.

title: Can Food Actually Be Medicine? These Doctors Say Yes
summary: Prescribing produce, crafting meals: More medical schools are teaching students how to cook and use food as a tool for treating patients.

title: The Jump Rope Queen of Beverly Hills
summary: Annie Judis, 82, gets a kick out of defying expectations about what the body can do.

title: How and When to Watch NASA’s Artemis II Splash Down in Pacific Ocean
summary: Here’s what you need to know about Artemis II and the splashdown of the Orion capsule.

title: See Photos From All 10 Days of NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission
summary: Over 10 days, the astronauts of NASA’s lunar flyby mission have achieved the near impossible.

title: My 5 Favorite Places for Art in London
summary: Our critic Jason Farago shares what you shouldn’t miss in a city undergoing a palpable cultural renewal.

title: Trump Is Turning America Into a Psychotic State
summary: What are the implications if the government of the world’s most powerful country is chaotic in its thinking and not reliably in touch with reality?

title: Why Republicans Will Not Run Away From Trump
summary: Without Trump on the midterms ballot, how will his party fare?

title: You Can’t Use This A.I.
summary: Claude Mythos Preview is dangerous, Anthropic said. We explain the risks.

title: Should Churches Remove Art by a Priest Accused of Sexual Abuse?
summary: The Rev. Marko Ivan Rupnik has installed mosaics across the Roman Catholic world, including at the Vatican. After nuns accused him of abuse, some want his work removed.

title: To Fill Air Traffic Controller Shortage, F.A.A. Turns to Gamers
summary: Avid players of video games have emerged as a target demographic for recruiters at a range of federal agencies, including the military and the Department of Homeland Security.

title: State Judges Turn to Guns in New Era of Judicial Threats
summary: A Times examination reveals thousands of threats against state judges, in addition to assaults and fatal attacks. Judges say local law enforcement agencies often can’t offer adequate protection.

title: How Authors and Readers Feel About the ‘Shy Girl’ Cancellation
summary: Major publishing houses risk unwittingly putting out books generated with A.I. tools. Authors and readers are frustrated, nervous and grasping for solutions.
